title: Smoky Argentinian Fish Pilaf
description: A savory, dense pilaf from Argentina featuring smoky paprika and tender fish, perfect for a date-night dinner in a tandoor clay pot.

ingredients.name: Smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: use high-quality, smoky paprika for authentic flavor

ingredients.name: Rice
ingredients.notes: short-grain rice works best for pilafs

instructions: Heat the tandoor clay pot over medium-high heat.
Add the olive oil and swirl to coat the bottom of the pot.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the cumin and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the rice and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the rice is lightly toasted.
Add 2 cups of water to the pot and bring to a boil.
Add the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Add the cod fillets to the pot, skin side up (if they have skin).
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated tandoor clay oven or a conventional oven at 400°F (200°C).
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and the rice is tender.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and garnish with chopped fresh parsley.
Serve hot, directly from the clay pot.
